title: [lichen_l2.c:659-672 + lora_l2.c] enable(true) doesn't init replay_table on re-init
description:
Cross-file bug: When lichen_l2_enable(true) re-initializes link_ctx (lines 670-672), it does NOT call lichen_replay_table_init(&replay_table). Compare to lichen_l2_iface_init() which calls both lichen_link_init() at line 885 AND lichen_replay_table_init() at line 886.

Sequence:
1. iface_init() runs - inits link_ctx + replay_table
2. enable(false) runs - clears link_ctx_initialized, cleans up link_ctx, reinits replay_table (line 721)
3. enable(true) runs - link_ctx_initialized is false, re-inits link_ctx but NOT replay_table

The disable path at line 721 calls lichen_replay_table_init() to clear stale windows. But if the table's init function does more than zeroing (e.g., sets magic values), using the table without proper init could cause undefined behavior.

Fix: Add lichen_replay_table_init(&replay_table) after lichen_link_init() at line 672, matching the init path.
close_reason: Fixed: Added lichen_replay_table_init() call in enable(true) re-init path to match iface_init() behavior
